instruments.note: ES numbers scaled from NQ by typical range ratio (~4x). Literal 32-tick ES target = 8 ES points = much bigger move than 8 NQ points. Override in indicator inputs if you want literal 32t on ES.

edge.name: Price Reactions at Ranked Structural Levels
edge.thesis: NY session NQ respects ranked structural levels in BOTH directions: reactions AWAY from a level (reaction wicks larger than the 32-tick target) and the pull INTO a powerful level once price is within the approach window. The stronger the level, the more it earns trades toward and away from it. Wait for price to engage a ranked level, take the fixed-tick trade, exit at target. Patience is the edge.

IB.role: THE set play — only breakout play traded
IB.rule: IB = 09:30-10:30 range. N consecutive closes beyond IBH/IBL (default 2) = acceptance. Enter break direction on the acceptance close. Max 1 signal per side per day.

MR.role: default play — everything that is not the IB break
MR.rule: Price approaches a cluster with rank >= minimum (default 4). Wick tags the level (within tolerance), does NOT pierce deeper than stop distance, close rejects back on the original side with close in the rejection zone (top 40% of bar for longs, bottom 40% for shorts). Enter reaction direction.
MR.confirm: volume footprint on DOM/orderflow platform: bottom-heavy = buyers = long reaction, top-heavy = sellers = short reaction. Pine cannot see bid/ask footprint — indicator proxies with close-position + optional relative volume. Footprint stays a manual eyes-on check.
MR.stop: standard; a CLOSE through the level = level broken, stand down on that level

BRT.role: continuation play — the punch-through case. OFF by default in the indicator until validated.
BRT.rule: Level punched (confirmed close beyond by escape margin), then price retests it from the other side within the watch window and holds on close. Enter continuation using the broken level as the launch point. One shot per level per escape.

APP.role: toward play — ride INTO a powerful level once close. Strength scales the pull.
APP.rule: A cluster with rank >= min_rank_approach sits roughly one target distance ahead (approach window: 1.0-1.5x target points). Price traveling toward it (with-move close, not fresh-rejected at it). Enter toward the level; target lands at/just in front of it. Logic: 32t in front of a strong level nearly always gets wicked; when it doesn't, the trade quality still justified the stop.
APP.stop: standard; NEVER hold through the level — the level is the exit. Reaction there is a new decision (possible MR the other way).
APP.status: manual play for now — journal as APP; indicator signal + backtest validation queued

direction_rules: Price falls from above into support cluster + rejection wick + close back above = LONG reaction
Price rises from below into resistance cluster + rejection wick + close back below = SHORT reaction
Wick pierces beyond stop distance = punched, no MR entry — becomes a BRT watch
OPEN QUESTION: v1 JSON said 'price from above at a level = SHORT'. That contradicts bounce logic AND the v1 live-example trades. Indicator implements reaction logic above. Confirm or flip.

level_system.static_ranking: rank = scope + kind. Scope: session 1, day 3, week 5. Kind: POC 5, VAH/VAL 3, period H/L 3, HVN 2, LVN 1. Examples: pwPOC=10, pwVAH=8, pdPOC=8, pdVAH=6, NY-session POC=6, session VAH=4, session LVN=2.
level_system.clustering: levels within cluster width (25% of target distance: 2.0 pts NQ / 0.5 pts ES) merge into one cluster. Cluster rank = SUM of member ranks. Cluster price = rank-weighted mean. Confluence literally adds up.
level_system.break_rule: confirmed close through a cluster beyond escape margin = broken. Never fade a broken level twice. Broken level = BRT launch-pad candidate.

rules.enforced_by: trader. Indicator counts signals vs plan (SIGNALS row goes red at plan) but never blocks. Market open = opportunities.

rules.value: fixed stop ticks (48t NQ / 12t ES), no exceptions
fixed target ticks (32t NQ / 8t ES), exit at target, never hold for more
price comes to the level; never chase
11:30-13:30 ET — toggle exists, OFF by default
UNDEFINED — SPEC GAP. Define a hard stand-down number before scaling.

expectancy_math.v1_table_correction: v1 daily_reality used loss = target size. Real loss at a 12-pt stop is 1.5x the win. Corrected loss column in account_plan below. v1 also listed 1 NQ risk as 16% — even by its own numbers ($160/$2000) that is 8%.

account_plan.scaling_strategy: Jump contracts when milestone hit — EDIT to your own account
account_plan.flag: SAMPLE numbers. This is the file the app and mentor read live — make it YOUR plan before trading.

indicator.name: Skuld Unified v1

modes.structure: chart TF >= switch TF (default 1H): weekly+daily ranked cluster map + developing week profile. No signals.
modes.execution: chart TF < switch TF: session VP histogram, session VWAP ±1σ/±2σ, ONH/ONL, IB lines, full cluster map (sessions included), IB/MR/BRT signals any time bars print (RTH-only toggle off by default), trade plan lines that terminate on TP/SL with outcome stamp, signals-vs-plan counter (display only).

indicator.alerts: single 'Any alert() function call' alert covers every signal with entry/stop/target text
indicator.settings: all colors, line styles, widths per scope, VP rows/width/transparency, sessions, ranks, widths, discipline caps exposed as inputs

pre_market: Open execution chart (5m/15m) — cluster map + stars auto-built from prior week/day/session profiles
Check structure chart (1H/4H) — weekly/daily map, note any ★★★★+ cluster within one day's range
Note ONH/ONL, gap vs prior settle
Rank check: which clusters sit within 25% width of each other (auto-merged, higher stars)
Confirm max-3 counter reset, alerts armed
during_session: Wait. Price comes to a starred cluster or the IB acceptance fires
Footprint check on DOM at the touch (manual — indicator proxy is not a footprint)
Take signal, orders at plan lines: entry, fixed stop, fixed target
Exit at target. Screenshot. Journal line.
Close through a level = broken: no re-fade, watch for BRT diamond
3 trades done = done. Panel says DONE.
end_of_day: Journal: which clusters held vs broke, star-rank vs outcome
Weekly: re-rank — do ★★★★+ clusters outperform ★★? Adjust min rank input from data, not feel

per_day: levels held vs broke by star rank

open_items: Define YOUR daily max loss before trading real size — the app surfaces it but will not invent it.
